primaryStudies.citation: Lee JV, Burgess MS. Safety of Intravenous Infusion of BPC157 in Humans: A Pilot Study. J Pers Med. 2025;15(3):131.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: IV infusions up to 20mg well tolerated; no adverse events; no changes in cardiac, hepatic, renal, or metabolic biomarkers

primaryStudies.citation: Vasireddi N, et al. Emerging Use of BPC-157 in Orthopaedic Sports Medicine: A Systematic Review. Am J Sports Med. 2025.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: BPC-157 enhances GHR expression, angiogenesis pathways, reduces inflammatory cytokines; improves outcomes in muscle, tendon, ligament, bone injuries in preclinical models

primaryStudies.citation: Lee JV, et al. Pilot Study of BPC-157 Effects on Interstitial Cystitis Symptoms. Altern Ther Health Med. 2024;30(4):100-105.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Symptom improvement observed; small pilot study

croatianResearchGroup.citation: Sikiric P, Seiwerth S, Rucman R, et al. Brain-gut Axis and Pentadecapeptide BPC 157: Gastrointestinal and Brain Healing Peptide. Curr Neuropharmacol. 2016;14(8):857-865.
croatianResearchGroup.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of BPC-157 brain-gut axis effects; dopaminergic system modulation; neuroprotection mechanisms

croatianResearchGroup.citation: Sikiric P, et al. Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157: novel therapy in gastrointestinal tract. Curr Pharm Des. 2011;17(16):1612-1632.
croatianResearchGroup.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of BPC-157 gastrointestinal effects; healing of various GI lesions; cytoprotection mechanisms

croatianResearchGroup.citation: Sikiric P, et al. BPC 157 and Standard Angiogenic Growth Factors. Gastrointestinal Tract Healing, Lessons from Tendon, Ligament, Muscle and Bone Healing. Curr Pharm Des. 2018;24(18):1972-1989.
croatianResearchGroup.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of BPC-157 angiogenic mechanisms across tissue types; implements angiogenic effect in healing

croatianResearchGroup.citation: Sikiric P, et al. Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157-NO-system relation. Curr Pharm Des. 2014;20(7):1126-1135.
croatianResearchGroup.keyFindings: BPC-157 interacts with nitric oxide system; modulates NO synthesis for vascular and healing effects

croatianResearchGroup.citation: Sikiric P, Seiwerth S, Rucman R, et al. Modulatory effect of gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 on angiogenesis in muscle and tendon healing. J Physiol Pharmacol. 2010;61(2):191-194.
croatianResearchGroup.keyFindings: Stimulates angiogenesis by upregulating VEGF expression; related to healing process in vivo

croatianResearchGroup.citation: Sikiric P, et al. Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and the central nervous system. Curr Neuropharmacol. 2022;20(4):690-708.
croatianResearchGroup.keyFindings: Comprehensive CNS effects review; neuroprotection; dopaminergic, serotonergic, GABAergic system interactions

croatianResearchGroup.citation: Sikiric P, et al. Counteraction of Perforated Cecum Lesions and Resultant Multiple Organ Dysfunction Syndrome by Pentadecapeptide BPC 157. J Pharmacol Sci. 2008;108(2):212-223.
croatianResearchGroup.keyFindings: BPC-157 attenuated multiple organ failure; improved survival; reduced inflammatory markers

croatianResearchGroup.citation: Sikiric P, et al. Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 in trials for inflammatory bowel disease (PL-10, PLD-116, PL 14736, Pliva, Croatia). Full and partial agonism on dopamine D2 receptor. J Physiol Pharmacol. 2006;57 Suppl 9:61-72.
croatianResearchGroup.keyFindings: Summary of IBD clinical trial development; dopamine receptor agonism mechanism

croatianResearchGroup.citation: Sikiric P, et al. Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 attenuates chronic amphetamine-induced behavior disturbances. Acta Pharmacol Sin. 2002;23(5):412-422.
croatianResearchGroup.keyFindings: Attenuated amphetamine-induced behavioral disturbances; dopaminergic modulation

croatianResearchGroup.citation: Sikiric P, et al. Toxicity by NSAIDs. Counteraction by stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157. Curr Pharm Des. 2013;19(1):76-83.
croatianResearchGroup.keyFindings: BPC-157 counteracts NSAID-induced GI lesions, hepatotoxicity, nephrotoxicity in animal models

croatianResearchGroup.citation: Sikiric P, et al. Stable Gastric Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 and Wound Healing. Front Pharmacol. 2022;13:813504.
croatianResearchGroup.keyFindings: Comprehensive wound healing review; skin, cornea, GI tract healing mechanisms

croatianResearchGroup.citation: Seiwerth S, Sikiric P, et al. BPC 157's effects on healing. J Physiol Paris. 2001;95(1-6):529-534.
croatianResearchGroup.keyFindings: Early comprehensive review of healing effects across tissue types

taiwaneseResearch.citation: Chang CH, Tsai WC, Lin MS, et al. The promoting effect of pentadecapeptide BPC 157 on tendon healing involves tendon outgrowth, cell survival, and cell migration. J Appl Physiol. 2011;110(3):774-780.
taiwaneseResearch.keyFindings: Promotes tendon fibroblast outgrowth, cell survival under stress, dose-dependent migration via FAK-paxillin pathway

taiwaneseResearch.citation: Chang CH, Tsai WC, Hsu YH, Pang JH. Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 enhances the growth hormone receptor expression in tendon fibroblasts. Molecules. 2014;19(11):19066-19077.
taiwaneseResearch.keyFindings: BPC-157 upregulates growth hormone receptor expression; JAK2/STAT pathway activation

taiwaneseResearch.citation: Hsieh MJ, Liu HT, Wang CN, et al. Therapeutic potential of pro-angiogenic BPC157 is associated with VEGFR2 activation and up-regulation. J Mol Med. 2017;95(3):323-333.
taiwaneseResearch.keyFindings: BPC-157 activates VEGFR2; promotes angiogenesis via PI3K-Akt-eNOS pathway; accelerates ischemic wound healing

taiwaneseResearch.citation: Huang T, Zhang K, Sun L, et al. Body protection compound-157 enhances alkali-burn wound healing in vivo and promotes proliferation, migration, and angiogenesis in vitro. Drug Des Devel Ther. 2015;9:2485-2499.
taiwaneseResearch.keyFindings: BPC-157 accelerated corneal wound healing; promoted endothelial cell proliferation and migration

chineseResearch.citation: Wang XY, Qu M, Duan R, et al. Cytoprotective mechanism of the novel gastric peptide BPC157 in gastrointestinal tract and liver. World J Gastroenterol. 2019;25(28):3851-3863.
chineseResearch.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of BPC-157 cytoprotective mechanisms in GI and liver; cell signaling pathways

chineseResearch.citation: Xu C, Sun L, Ren F, et al. Preclinical safety evaluation of body protection compound-157, a potential drug for treating various wounds. Regul Toxicol Pharmacol. 2016;78:21-29.
chineseResearch.keyFindings: Comprehensive preclinical safety assessment; no acute toxicity, genotoxicity, or local irritation observed

chineseResearch.citation: Sun L, Zhang K, Huang T, et al. BPC157 protects HUVECs against high glucose-induced injury via downregulating PKC pathway. Biomed Pharmacother. 2016;83:1291-1297.
chineseResearch.keyFindings: BPC-157 protected endothelial cells from hyperglycemia-induced damage via PKC pathway modulation

chineseResearch.citation: Xue XC, Wu YJ, Gao MT, et al. Protective effects of pentadecapeptide BPC 157 on gastric ulcer in rats. World J Gastroenterol. 2004;10(7):1032-1036.
chineseResearch.keyFindings: BPC-157 accelerated gastric ulcer healing; increased prostaglandin E2 and growth factors

europeanResearch.citation: Gwyer D, Wragg NM, Wilson SL. Gastric pentadecapeptide body protection compound BPC 157 and its role in accelerating musculoskeletal soft tissue healing. Cell Tissue Res. 2019;377(2):153-159.
europeanResearch.keyFindings: Independent UK review of BPC-157 musculoskeletal healing evidence; summarizes mechanisms and limitations

europeanResearch.citation: Tkalcevic VI, Cuzic S, Brajsa K, et al. Enhancement by PL 14736 of granulation and collagen organization in healing wounds and the potential role of egr-1 expression. Eur J Pharmacol. 2007;570(1-3):212-221.
europeanResearch.keyFindings: BPC-157 (PL 14736) enhanced wound granulation tissue formation and collagen organization via Egr-1 pathway

europeanResearch.citation: Cerovecki T, Bojanic I, Brcic L, et al. Pentadecapeptide BPC 157 (PL 14736) improves ligament healing in the rat. J Orthop Res. 2010;28(9):1155-1161.
europeanResearch.keyFindings: BPC-157 improved ligament healing; increased collagen type I expression; improved biomechanical properties

europeanResearch.citation: Staresinic M, Petrovic I, Novinscak T, et al. Effective therapy of transected quadriceps muscle in rat: Gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157. J Orthop Res. 2006;24(5):1109-1117.
europeanResearch.keyFindings: BPC-157 improved functional recovery and muscle healing; increased myofiber density

europeanResearch.citation: Novinscak T, Brcic L, Staresinic M, et al. Gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 as an effective therapy for muscle crush injury in the rat. Surg Today. 2008;38(8):716-725.
europeanResearch.keyFindings: BPC-157 accelerated muscle healing after crush injury; improved functional outcomes

japaneseKoreanResearch.citation: Kang EA, Han YM, An JM, et al. BPC157 as Potential Agent for Protection Against Oxidative Stress, Free Radical-Mediated Injury and Inflammation: A Systematic Review. Int J Mol Sci. 2019;20(20):5182.
japaneseKoreanResearch.keyFindings: Korean systematic review of BPC-157 antioxidant and anti-inflammatory mechanisms; comprehensive pathway analysis

japaneseKoreanResearch.citation: Vukojevic J, Siroglavic M, Kasnik K, et al. Rat inferior caval vein (IVC) ligation and BPC 157. A new rat IVC syndrome. Vasc Pharmacol. 2018;109:47-56.
japaneseKoreanResearch.keyFindings: BPC-157 prevented venous hypertension complications; maintained vascular integrity

recentReviews2024_2025.citation: Regeneration or Risk? A Narrative Review of BPC-157 Safety and Efficacy. PMC12446177. 2025.
recentReviews2024_2025.keyFindings: No clinical safety data found; warns about lack of human evidence; recommends caution

recentReviews2024_2025.citation: BPC-157 in Sports Medicine: Current State of Evidence. PMC12313605. 2025.
recentReviews2024_2025.keyFindings: Sports medicine applications review; recommends against clinical use pending human trials

recentReviews2024_2025.citation: Sikiric P, et al. Pleiotropic activity and neurotransmitter relations of BPC 157. PMC11053547. 2024.
recentReviews2024_2025.keyFindings: Pleiotropic activity; dopamine, serotonin, GABA, NO system interactions; neuroprotection

trials.title: Safety, Tolerability and Pharmacokinetics of Single and Multiple Ascending Doses of PL 14736 in Healthy Male Subjects
trials.notes: Cancelled in 2016; no results ever published; enrollment completed but data not disclosed

trials.title: Multiple Phase I/II trials for IBD (PL-10, PLD-116)
trials.notes: Referenced in 2006 review paper; UC and Crohn's trials mentioned; full data never published

evidenceSummary.regulatoryStatus: Not approved; FDA Category 2 bulk drug (2023); banned in sports (WADA/USADA)
evidenceSummary.keyLimitation: Decades of promising animal data but only 2-3 small pilot human studies; cancelled Phase I trial; majority of research from single Croatian group
evidenceSummary.independentReplication: Limited - Taiwanese and Chinese groups have published supportive findings; UK independent review exists
evidenceSummary.researchConcentration: Estimated 80%+ of publications from Zagreb research group (Sikiric et al.)

globalResearchSources.pubmed: Primary source - all major studies indexed
globalResearchSources.cnki: Chinese studies on wound healing and cytoprotection
globalResearchSources.croatiaDatabases: Primary research origin - University of Zagreb
